h2h_summary: In their 3 all-time meetings, the United States has won 2 matches and drawn 1, while Bosnia and Herzegovina has won 0.
wc_history_home: The USMNT are appearing in their 12th World Cup and their best result in the modern era was a quarter-final finish in 2002, while their best ever result was a semi-final in the inaugural 1930 tournament; as co-hosts in 2026 they face immense pressure to equal or surpass that 2002 peak.
wc_history_away: Bosnia and Herzegovina made their only previous World Cup appearance at Brazil 2014, winning their opening group game 3-1 against Iran but narrowly missing the round of 16 after losses to Argentina and Nigeria.
group_context: Round of 32 knockout — no draws; level after 90 goes to extra time and penalties. Winner advances to the Round of 16.
strengths_home: Home crowd advantage at SoFi Stadium provides a significant psychological and atmospheric edge
Deep European-based squad with elite club experience through Pulisic, McKennie, Adams, and Balogun
High-energy pressing system under Pochettino creates turnovers and generates attacking transitions
strengths_away: Experienced striking partnership with Dzeko and Demirovic providing both hold-up play and pressing
Mental fortitude and big-game experience after eliminating both Wales and Italy on penalties in qualifying
Dangerous from set pieces and crosses with Dzeko's aerial presence in the box
team_style_home: The USMNT under Mauricio Pochettino employ a high-pressing 3-4-2-1/3-2-5 hybrid that shifts to a 4-2-3-1 defensively, using wing-backs for width and relying on Pulisic's movement and Balogun's finishing to create and convert chances.
team_style_away: Bosnia and Herzegovina play a compact 4-4-2 or 4-2-3-1, relying on physicality, direct play, quick counter-attacks, and set-piece threat with Edin Dzeko as the focal point of their attack.
recent_news: Christian Pulisic says he’s fit to play full match for USMNT vs. Bosnia after recovering from a calf injury. The United States secured first place in Group D with wins over Paraguay (4-1) and Australia (2-0).
context: Round of 32 knockout — no draws; level after 90 goes to extra time and penalties. Winner advances to the Round of 16.
